data.title: Disc Medicine GAAP EPS of -$3.96 beats by $1.01
data.description:
• Disc Medicine press release (NASDAQ:IRON): FY GAAP EPS of -$3.96 beats by $1.01.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities were $489.9 million as of December 31, 2024, compared to $360.4 million as of December 31, 2023.
  • We expect that our existing cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of December 31, 2024, together with the estimated net proceeds of $243.3 million from our underwritten public offering completed in January 2025, will be sufficient to fund operational plans into 2028.

data.title: Entegris set to join S&P MidCap 400, shares up 6% in aftermarket trading
data.description:
• Entegris (NASDAQ:ENTG) will replace Arcadium Lithium (ALTM) in the S&P MidCap 400 effective prior to the opening of trading on Thursday, March 6.
  • Rio Tinto plc (RIO) is acquiring Arcadium Lithium in a deal expected to be completed soon.
  • Entegris (NASDAQ:ENTG) shares rose +6.85% to $104.16 in Thursday's post-market trading.

data.title: Xencor GAAP EPS of -$0.62 beats by $0.19, revenue of $52.8M beats by $34.33M
data.description:
• Xencor press release (NASDAQ:XNCR): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.62 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $52.8M (+3.6% Y/Y) beats by $34.33M.

data.title: Turkcell Iletisim reports FY results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Turkcell Iletisim press release (NYSE:TKC): FY Revenue of TRY 166.67B (+7.8% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA increased 10.2%, leading to an EBITDA margin of 41.9%, marking a yearly improvement of 0.9pp; EBIT up 22.5%, resulting in an EBIT margin of 13.3%
  • Net income up 29.8% to TRY 23.5 billion, including the sale of subsidiaries in Ukraine
  • Strong free cash flow generation of TRY 7.3 billion; net leverage level at 0.14x; net short FX position of $124 million in line with our neutral FX definition, which is between plus and minus $200 million.
  • 2025 guidance: revenue growth target of between 7%-9%, data center and cloud revenue growth target of between 32%-34%, EBITDA margin target of between 41%-42%, and operational capex over sales ratio target of around 24%.

data.title: VSE beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• VSE press release (NASDAQ:VSEC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.90 beats by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $299M (+27.1% Y/Y) beats by $9.03M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $39.5 million increased 26%.
  • The company generated $55 million of operating cash flow and $52 million of free cash flow for the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • As of December 31, 2024, the company had $223 million in cash and unused commitment availability under its $350 million revolving credit facility maturing in October 2026.

  • AVIATION GUIDANCE

    • Aviation segment full year 2025 revenue growth is expected to be 35% to 40%, as compared to the prior year. Full year revenue growth includes revenue contributions from the TCI and Kellstrom acquisitions.
    • Aviation segment full year 2025 Adjusted EBITDA margin is expected to be between 15.5% and 16.5%. This includes the near-term margin dilution impact from the TCI and Kellstrom acquisitions.

    FLEET GUIDANCE

    • The Company will not be providing guidance due to the recently announced sale of the Fleet segment.

data.title: HSBC reduces workforce at China digital wealth unit Pinnacle - report
data.description:
HSBC (OTCPK:HBCYF) (NYSE:HSBC) is reducing the workforce at its China digital wealth business Pinnacle by nearly half, or about 900 people, Reuters reported citing sources familiar with the matter.

The move is seen as a sharp reversal of the bank's ambition for the unit as part of its expansion plans in China, and underscores the challenges the Asia-focused bank faces to boost growth.

Pinnacle, launched in 2020, sells insurance and fund products through a digital platform in China. The unit employed about 2,100 people at two of its main units as of the end of June last year, according to the report.

The staff reduction involves layoffs, natural attrition, and transfers to other units within the banking group in China, said the two sources.

Earlier this year, HSBC (NYSE:HSBC) said it would wind down its mergers and acquisitions and equity capital markets businesses in Europe, the UK, and the U.S. as part of a significant restructuring strategy.

The London-headquartered bank has revealed plans to slash $3B in cost and reorganize into four new divisions.
